# nla

NLA provides a simple set of functions to aid in performing non-linear
averaging.

$$\overline{f(X)} \neq f\left( \overline{X} \right)$$

## Installation

You can install the development version of `nla` from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("fwimp/nla")
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`decimalplaces()`](https://fwimp.github.io/nla/reference/decimalplaces.md)
  : Find the decimal places in a float
- [`find_dps()`](https://fwimp.github.io/nla/reference/find_dps.md) :
  Find the decimal places in a vector of floats
- [`nla_tpc_empirical()`](https://fwimp.github.io/nla/reference/nla_tpc_empirical.md)
  : Perform nonlinear averaging of a temperature-dependent variable
  across a range of temperatures from empirically-derived data
